text: Re search: I implemented the 'easy and cheap' version where the costs were mostly up-front (processing each tweet archive with LLMs once) and running the actual search is very fast and basically free (nearest-neighbour search between semantic embeddings.)

raw.full_text: Re search: I implemented the 'easy and cheap' version where the costs were mostly up-front (processing each tweet archive with LLMs once) and running the actual search is very fast and basically free (nearest-neighbour search between semantic embeddings.)

text: Context TL;DR: CA is a relatively small dataset, so this project serves as a demo/pointer to "matchmaking tech" I would like to see in the future, with the dream being that everyone can do the work they love with people they match well with. ("live markets", for the job market.)

raw.full_text: Context TL;DR: CA is a relatively small dataset, so this project serves as a demo/pointer to "matchmaking tech" I would like to see in the future, with the dream being that everyone can do the work they love with people they match well with. ("live markets", for the job market.)

text: This does result in some weirdness, for example "basketball coach" shows @ChrischipMonk first (due to being a coach), before @jakob_reinwald (likely the only basketball fan in the archive from what I can tell)

raw.full_text: This does result in some weirdness, for example "basketball coach" shows @ChrischipMonk first (due to being a coach), before @jakob_reinwald (likely the only basketball fan in the archive from what I can tell)

text: I think I've worked on this enough now to check if there's value in it in this state; if there's interest I'd be happy to work on it more (e.g. by reworking the search). I expect to maintain this out of pocket for the foreseeable future

text:
Casa Tilo!

So much beauty in the house, the people, the energy, the gorgeous forest we were all embedded in. So much grace and skill in the hosting. Landing was easy and gentle, much easier than most other communities I've stayed with (actually a remarkable achievement and a big thing for me & my bf). I'm excited to see this project grow, and excited to go back ❤️☀️

@RichDecibels and @LombardoNati are making magic ✨

Also I'm hyped to finally have my own @danallison sticker

text: @cavi_jointleman I don't but the code is open source on my github. I built a pipeline that pulls in your archive from CA, feeds it to an LLM (in bits if there's too much context which there usually is) to create one cohesive summary, get semantic embeddings for the summary...

raw.full_text: @cavi_jointleman I don't but the code is open source on my github. I built a pipeline that pulls in your archive from CA, feeds it to an LLM (in bits if there's too much context which there usually is) to create one cohesive summary, get semantic embeddings for the summary...

text: @cavi_jointleman ...stores the summary and the embeddings in firebase and then the the search is just a super cheap search vector search with the embedding of the query over each summary. Writing this from memory after 4 months so maybe missing something...

raw.full_text: @cavi_jointleman ...stores the summary and the embeddings in firebase and then the the search is just a super cheap search vector search with the embedding of the query over each summary. Writing this from memory after 4 months so maybe missing something...

text: @cavi_jointleman Lots of room to optimise! Like making the ranking better would be cool but I wanted to prioritise making the query itself cheap and fast.

raw.full_text: @cavi_jointleman Lots of room to optimise! Like making the ranking better would be cool but I wanted to prioritise making the query itself cheap and fast.
